mechanism_narrative: Rising real sales (RSAFS) signal strengthening consumer demand and corporate revenue growth, which directly translates to higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously without threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 368861M in Nov 2006 reflects robust consumer spending
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or correlation becomes negative for more than 3 consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, monetary tightening restricts credit availability and raises discount rates, suppressing equity valuations. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as market participants reprrice risk.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.25% in Nov 2006 indicates restrictive policy stance
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals labor market tightening and wage pressure, which reduces corporate profit margins and increases cost inflation. The joint deterioration of labor supply and margin compression drives negative equity returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 4.5% AND CPIAUCSL rises > 0.4% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.5% and CPIAUCSL at 201.7 in Nov 2006
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with wage-price spiral risk
failure_condition: When both triggers are met, S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 3-year regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above its 12-month trend indicates robust supply-side capacity utilization and productivity gains, supporting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. This mechanism operates as a continuous trend signal.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 112.32 in Nov 2006 reflects manufacturing strength
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO growth rate and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or the relationship reverses sign for more than 2 consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining below 1400K units signals weakening residential demand and construction activity, which cascades into reduced consumer wealth, lower durable goods orders, and negative equity returns over 60 trading days. Activation occurs when HOUST falls below the threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1486K in Oct 2006 approaching weakness threshold
failure_condition: When HOUST < 1400K, S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerating below 100K new jobs per month signals labor market weakening and reduced consumer income growth. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as growth expectations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 136018K in Nov 2006 with recent deceleration
failure_condition: When PAYEMS growth < 100K, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.015 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 5.0%) combined with rising consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL > 202.0) signals restrictive monetary policy in an inflationary environment. This dual constraint reduces real purchasing power and equity valuations, driving negative returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.25% and CPIAUCSL at 201.7 in Nov 2006
failure_condition: When both triggers are met, S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 18-month regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) acceleration above 3.0% annualized signals robust economic expansion and rising corporate earnings expectations. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as growth momentum sustains equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11450.5B in Q3 2006 reflects solid economic expansion
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth rate and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or correlation becomes negative for more than 2 consecutive quarters.
